title: One number everywhere
intro: This landscape is not rock — it is electric potential V, drawn as height. V is one plain number at every point; the contour rings are where that number is equal. Drop balls and watch them roll. To win, land 3 balls in the flagged home basin — but there is a decoy valley, and the ridge between them is invisible unless you read the contours.
explain: A ball rolls along −∇V: straight DOWN the local slope, perpendicular to every contour line it crosses — not straight at the deepest point. That is the whole trick: V itself has no direction (it is a scalar field, one number per point), while its gradient ∇V is a vector you get for free by asking 'which way is downhill, and how steep?'. Where contours crowd together the slope — the electric field — is strong. Real charges do exactly this: E = −∇V, which is why field lines always cross equipotentials at right angles.

prediction.prompt: A ball is dropped at the white marker. Sketch the path it will roll.
